description_long: This is a compact handheld two-way radio transceiver, commonly known as a walkie-talkie. It features a prominent black antenna extending from the top, a rectangular body with a textured grip, and a front-facing illuminated display screen with red backlighting. Below the screen, there is a numeric keypad and several physical buttons for channel and function selection, as well as a speaker grille. The top of the device includes two rotary knobs, likely for power/volume and channel selection, and a side button is visible for push-to-talk functionality. The casing appears to be made of durable black plastic, designed for rugged use in urban or outdoor environments.
description: A black handheld walkie-talkie with a long antenna, illuminated display, and physical keypad. It features rotary knobs and a rugged, textured body.
description_view_0: Front view showing the illuminated display, keypad, speaker grille, and part of the antenna.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back casing, battery compartment, and the full length of the antenna.
description_view_2: Rear-side view showing the battery compartment, side button, and rotary knobs on top.
description_view_3: Angled front view displaying the screen, keypad, and both rotary knobs, with the antenna prominently visible.
